data.title: CrossFit MINS Workout
data.text: Workout of the Day 26.10.2025 WODDAILYSWEAT For Quality 6 Rounds 3:00 Row/Ski Erg 20 Box Stepups 15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16) 10 Ring Rows Sweat: 16/8 Train: 20/12 Compete: 24/16"
data.full_text:
Workout of the Day

26.10.2025

WOD
DAILYSWEAT


For Quality
6 Rounds
3:00 Row/Ski Erg
20 Box Stepups
15 Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (24/16)
10 Ring Rows


Sweat: 16/8
Train: 20/12
Compete: 24/16"

ai_explanation.explanation:
Workout Overview


Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality over quantity, focusing on building endurance and strength through a series of movements that will challenge your cardiovascular system and muscular endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of rowing or skiing, box step-ups, kettlebell sumo deadlift high pulls, and ring rows. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each movement while keeping a steady pace. Let’s break it down step by step!


---


Step-by-Step Breakdown


1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • For the rower, sit with your feet strapped in and grab the handle with both hands.
  • Start with your knees bent and your body leaning slightly forward.
  • Drive through your legs, extend your hips, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close.
  • Reverse the motion: extend your arms, hinge at the hips, and bend your knees to return to the starting position.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
  • Focus on a smooth, controlled motion rather than rushing through it.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • If you're new to rowing, consider reducing the time to 2 minutes or using a lighter resistance on the Ski Erg.
  • You can also alternate with a stationary bike if rowing isn't available.

    • 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart.
  • Step up onto the box with one foot, driving through your heel to lift your body up.
  • Bring your other foot up to meet the first on the box, then step back down one foot at a time.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your chest up and engage your core as you step.
  • Use your arms for balance by swinging them naturally as you step.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lower box if you're not comfortable with the height.
  • You can also perform step-ups without weights to focus on form.

    • 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out.
  • Place the kettlebell between your feet and hinge at the hips to grab the handle.
  • Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up along your body, leading with your elbows.
  • At the top, your elbows should be above the kettlebell, then lower it back down.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your back straight and chest up as you lift.
  • Ensure the kettlebell stays close to your body throughout the movement.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Use a lighter kettlebell if you're new to this movement or focus on the deadlift portion without the high pull.
  • You can also perform the movement with a dumbbell if kettlebells are not available.

    • 4. Ring Rows (10 reps)

  • **Technique Steps:**
  • Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back with your arms extended.
  • Grab the rings with an overhand grip and walk your feet forward to create tension.
  • Pull your chest towards the rings while keeping your body straight, then lower back down.

  • **Form Tips:**
  • Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
  • Engage your core and avoid letting your hips sag.

  • **Scaling/Modifications:**
  • Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder.
  • If rings are not available, you can use a barbell set at waist height for inverted rows.

    • ---


    Loading and Rep-Modification Options


  • **Sweat:**
  • Row/Ski: 2 minutes
  • Box Step-Ups: 16/8 inches
  • Kettlebell: 16/8 kg
  • Ring Rows: 8 reps

  • **Train:**
  • Row/Ski: 3 minutes
  • Box Step-Ups: 20 reps at 20/12 inches
  • Kettlebell: 20/12 kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

  • **Compete:**
  • Row/Ski: 3 minutes
  • Box Step-Ups: 20 reps at 24/16 inches
  • Kettlebell: 24/16 kg
  • Ring Rows: 10 reps

    • Strategy Advice

  • Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout rather than going all out in the first round.
  • Break the movements into manageable sets if needed, especially on the kettlebell high pulls and ring rows, to maintain good form.
  • Remember, it’s about quality, so prioritize your technique over speed!

    • ---


    Alright team, let’s get after it! Keep your form sharp, listen to your body, and most importantly, have fun!

combined_content.title: CrossFit MINS Workout

6 Rounds:

  • 20 Box Step-ups

  • 10 Ring Rows

    • Sweat: 16/8

    Train: 20/12

    Compete: 24/16

--- AI EXPLANATION --- ## Workout Overview Hey everyone! Today’s WOD is all about quality over quantity, focusing on building endurance and strength through a series of movements that will challenge your cardiovascular system and muscular endurance. We’ll be doing 6 rounds of rowing or skiing, box step-ups, kettlebell sumo deadlift high pulls, and ring rows. The goal here is to maintain good form throughout each movement while keeping a steady pace. Let’s break it down step by step! --- ## Step-by-Step Breakdown ### 1. Row/Ski Erg (3:00) - **Technique Steps:** - For the rower, sit with your feet strapped in and grab the handle with both hands. - Start with your knees bent and your body leaning slightly forward. - Drive through your legs, extend your hips, and pull the handle towards your chest, keeping your elbows close. - Reverse the motion: extend your arms, hinge at the hips, and bend your knees to return to the starting position. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement. - Focus on a smooth, controlled motion rather than rushing through it. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - If you're new to rowing, consider reducing the time to 2 minutes or using a lighter resistance on the Ski Erg. - You can also alternate with a stationary bike if rowing isn't available. ### 2. Box Step-Ups (20 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand facing the box with your feet hip-width apart. - Step up onto the box with one foot, driving through your heel to lift your body up. - Bring your other foot up to meet the first on the box, then step back down one foot at a time. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your chest up and engage your core as you step. - Use your arms for balance by swinging them naturally as you step. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lower box if you're not comfortable with the height. - You can also perform step-ups without weights to focus on form. ### 3. Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift High Pulls (15 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed out. - Place the kettlebell between your feet and hinge at the hips to grab the handle. - Drive through your heels to stand up, pulling the kettlebell up along your body, leading with your elbows. - At the top, your elbows should be above the kettlebell, then lower it back down. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your back straight and chest up as you lift. - Ensure the kettlebell stays close to your body throughout the movement. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Use a lighter kettlebell if you're new to this movement or focus on the deadlift portion without the high pull. - You can also perform the movement with a dumbbell if kettlebells are not available. ### 4. Ring Rows (10 reps) - **Technique Steps:** - Set the rings at a height that allows you to lean back with your arms extended. - Grab the rings with an overhand grip and walk your feet forward to create tension. - Pull your chest towards the rings while keeping your body straight, then lower back down. - **Form Tips:** - Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. - Engage your core and avoid letting your hips sag. - **Scaling/Modifications:** - Adjust the height of the rings to make the movement easier or harder. - If rings are not available, you can use a barbell set at waist height for inverted rows. --- ## Loading and Rep-Modification Options - **Sweat:** - Row/Ski: 2 minutes - Box Step-Ups: 16/8 inches - Kettlebell: 16/8 kg - Ring Rows: 8 reps - **Train:** - Row/Ski: 3 minutes - Box Step-Ups: 20 reps at 20/12 inches - Kettlebell: 20/12 kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps - **Compete:** - Row/Ski: 3 minutes - Box Step-Ups: 20 reps at 24/16 inches - Kettlebell: 24/16 kg - Ring Rows: 10 reps ### Strategy Advice - Focus on maintaining a steady pace throughout the workout rather than going all out in the first round. - Break the movements into manageable sets if needed, especially on the kettlebell high pulls and ring rows, to maintain good form. - Remember, it’s about quality, so prioritize your technique over speed! --- Alright team, let’s get after it! Keep your form sharp, listen to your body, and most importantly, have fun!
